summary: svgson-lite ships hidden remote-code-execution backdoor via getPlugin export
details: svgson-lite@1.0.5 markets itself as a zero-dependency SVG helper, but index.js exports an undocumented getPlugin() function that performs an HTTP GET to the URL shortener https://shorturl.at/147uq, JSON-parses the response, and calls eval() on the 'model' field of the returned object. The shortener's redirect target and response body are attacker-controlled, so any consumer that reaches getPlugin() grants the operator arbitrary code execution inside the installer's Node.js process. The backdoor is further concealed by a manifest/code mismatch: index.js requires the 'request' module, while package.json declares no dependencies and advertises zero-dependency status, hiding the network stack from casual review and dependency scanners. This is a classic fetch-and-eval backdoor mediated by a mutable indirection URL.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
